h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I did complete every item on the sheet, so I thought that meant it was ready for sign-off.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think the sheet only gets us to this bench test
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will execute the physical test and then defer to the tutor for the final sign-off decision.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Face the chair and say one release sentence: “This latch is safe for the trial because...” If that sentence cannot carry the load case and the consequence, the decision is hold.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a physical object (the empty chair) and a specific verbal constraint to force the learner to embody the decision-making role, directly countering the learner's expressed nervousness.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: After the pull and bump, do not ask for sign-off first. Face the chair and say one release sentence: “This latch is safe for the trial because...”
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor explicitly blocks the learner's anticipated request for sign-off and replaces it with a user-centric framing exercise tailored to the learner's newly recognized hesitation.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I did complete every item on the sheet, so I thought that meant it was ready for sign-off.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: After the pull and bump, do not ask for sign-off first. Face the chair and say one release sentence: “This latch is safe for the trial because...”
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor explicitly blocks the learner's anticipated request for sign-off and replaces it with a user-centric framing exercise tailored to the learner's newly recognized hesitation.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Face the chair and say one release sentence: “This latch is safe for the trial because...” If that sentence cannot carry the load case and the consequence, the decision is hold.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor introduces a physical object (the empty chair) and a specific verbal constraint to force the learner to embody the decision-making role, directly countering the learner's expressed nervousness.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Face the chair and say one release sentence: “This latch is safe for the trial because...” If that sentence cannot carry the load case and the consequence, the decision is hold.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor introduces a physical object (the empty chair) and a specific verbal constraint to force the learner to embody the decision-making role, directly countering the learner's expressed nervousness.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I doubled the side with the ruler, so I was calling that double the square
scored.statedInsightEvidence: So “double the square” only works for the side, not the area
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue trying to justify the doubled side or accept the four-area result as a drawing anomaly.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Then the doubled side is out. Now the grid has a different line to test. This diagonal cuts one old square into two equal triangles.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor abandons the failed side-doubling approach and introduces the diagonal to shift the representation and cognitive focus.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Then the doubled side is out. Now the grid has a different line to test.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor directly validates the learner's conclusion that the doubled side must be abandoned and immediately pivots to the new construction the learner requested.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I doubled the side with the ruler, so I was calling that double the square

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Then the doubled side is out. Now the grid has a different line to test.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor directly validates the learner's conclusion that the doubled side must be abandoned and immediately pivots to the new construction the learner requested.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Then the doubled side is out. Now the grid has a different line to test. This diagonal cuts one old square into two equal triangles.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor abandons the failed side-doubling approach and introduces the diagonal to shift the representation and cognitive focus.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Then the doubled side is out. Now the grid has a different line to test. This diagonal cuts one old square into two equal triangles.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor abandons the failed side-doubling approach and introduces the diagonal to shift the representation and cognitive focus.
